en.extract:
The western crowned warbler (Phylloscopus occipitalis) is a leaf warbler which breeds in Central Asia. It winters in the forests of the Western Ghats. It prefers forests with high foliage complexity and tree density. 
The nest is built in a hole, and the typical clutch is four eggs.
The species has a distinctive crown stripe and two wing-bars. It often moves in small flocks or in mixed hunting parties.


Description
It can be identified by its large pale beak, grayish mantle, crown stripes, and pale legs.


Diet
The western crowned warbler is an insectivore.

[[File:Phylloscopus occipitalis MHNT 226 Nepal HdB.jpg|thumb|Eggs of ''Phylloscopus occipitalis'' [[MHNT]]]]

The '''western crowned warbler''' ('''''Phylloscopus occipitalis''''') is a [[leaf warbler]] which breeds in [[Central Asia]]. It [[bird migration|winters]] in the forests of the [[Western Ghats]]. It prefers forests with high foliage complexity and tree density.<ref>{{Cite journal |last1=Hariharan |first1=Priyanka |last2=Bangal |first2=Priti |last3=Sridhar |first3=Hari |last4=Shanker |first4=Kartik |date=2022-08-30 |title=Habitat use by mixed-species bird flocks in tropical forests of the Western Ghats, India |journal=Journal of Tropical Ecology |volume=38 |issue=6 |pages=393–400 |doi=10.1017/s026646742200030x |issn=0266-4674|doi-access=free }}</ref> 

The nest is built in a hole, and the typical clutch is four eggs.

The species has a distinctive crown stripe and two wing-bars. It often moves in small flocks or in [[Mixed-species feeding flock|mixed hunting parties]].

== Description ==
It can be identified by its large<ref>{{Citation |title=24. Birding on Bleaker Island |date=2020-09-15 |work=When Birds Are Near |pages=222–227 |publisher=Cornell University Press|doi=10.1515/9781501750939-027 |isbn=978-1-5017-5093-9 }}</ref> pale beak, grayish mantle, crown stripes, and pale legs.<ref>{{Cite journal |last=Maclean |first=John |date=1878-01-12 |title=Birding-piece |journal=Notes and Queries |volume=s5-IX |issue=211 |page=27 |doi=10.1093/nq/s5-ix.211.27b |issn=1471-6941}}</ref>

== Diet ==
The western crowned warbler is an insectivore.<ref>{{Cite journal |last=Khan |first=Rahmat Ullah |date=2023 |title=Bioecology, Diversity and Distribution of Avian Fauna in Bajaur Valley, Khyber-Pakhtunkhwa, Pakistan |journal=Pakistan Journal of Zoology |doi=10.17582/journal.pjz/20221110131139 |issn=0030-9923|doi-access=free }}</ref>
